body_txt: Some people are just very good at weaving stories together. They might do so to entertain or to give cleverly delivered morals . Sometimes they might do so in order to outright lie, overlapping with Consummate Liar and also Seamless Spontaneous Lie . Whatever the case, they're very adept at weaving together a narrative that they deliver, often audibly. They might even do this for a living. In fiction, authors and novelists are particularly prone to being able to weave a story on the spot. This is often the purview of the Cool Old Guy . Occasionally it might be used as part of a Somber Backstory Revelation (whether that of the person telling the story or not is variable) and might overlap with ...And That Little Boy Was Me . It is also common to Honorary Uncles and many a Friend to All Children . See also Scheherezade Gambit , where successful uses of that trope almost require one to be this. Subtrope of The Storyteller , the key distinction being that the Consummate Storyteller can either come up with the tale on the spot or have it be utterly believable, often to the point characters hearing the story might live it. See also Let Me Tell You a Story . This is in regards to In-Universe examples only.
